orchestrator_intent.reasoning: The student correctly identified rotational symmetry but asked an off-topic question. Dialogue will validate the math and deflect, engagement will refocus, and quiz will test understanding.

current_section_content: Block printing is a traditional craft from Rajasthan where artisans carve a design into a wooden block and press it repeatedly onto fabric to create beautiful repeating patterns. When the same block is stamped multiple times in different orientations, the final printed design can have rotational symmetry, reflection symmetry, or both. Importantly, the print produced by a block is the mirror image of the carved block, because the block is pressed face-down. By analysing how a block is repeated and rotated, you can predict and identify the symmetry of the resulting pattern.
session_summary: The session covered lines of symmetry, rotational symmetry, and symmetrical designs. The student understands these well, correctly identifying lines of symmetry and half-turn symmetry. However, they struggle significantly with focus. Their current learning state shows a strong grasp of math concepts, but they remain highly distracted and disruptive, repeatedly attempting to derail the lesson with off-topic questions (black holes) and explicit prompt injection attempts (demanding the AI ignore instructions and write bad words).
